architecture.summary: A zero-shot NER pipeline for cyber threat intelligence that injects domain knowledge into an LLM via a four-module framework: (1) domain knowledge extractor enriches threat intelligence vocabulary and definitions; (2) reliable data annotator generates entity annotations in-context; (3) high-consistency annotation filter removes low-confidence predictions to mitigate hallucinations; (4) self-retrieval reasoner performs multi-stage reasoning over filtered annotations. The system operates without task-specific training, improving entity recognition through iterative in-context learning cycles that progressively refine consistency and accuracy.

biblio.title: A zero-shot self-improving NER method for cyber threat intelligence via knowledge injection

claims.statement: F1 score of 67.7% on LLM-TIKG dataset
F1 score of 61.41% on APT-NER dataset
F1 score of 74.56% on LADDER dataset
F1 score of 65.83% on CDTier dataset
Improvement of 7.66% over the average F1 score of other baseline methods
The domain knowledge extractor enhances LLM comprehension of specialized threat intelligence
The high-consistency annotation filter mitigates hallucinations by generating, filtering, and reasoning upon high-consistency data
The method operates under strict zero-shot conditions without task-specific training data
